title: Context API and Advanced State Management

learningObjectives.text: Explain providing and consuming context using a new example and justify the result.
Compare local versus global state using a new example and justify the result.
Compare ui versus remote state using a new example and justify the result.
Explain choosing state placement using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in context api and advanced state management recur throughout later React work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **Context and Advanced State Management** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Providing and consuming context** → **Local versus global state** → **UI versus remote state** → **Choosing state placement**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Providing and consuming context
sections.body: A context provider supplies a value to descendants; consumers read the nearest provider. Context transports data but does not organize every update automatically.

sections.title: Local versus global state
sections.body: Local state serves one instance or nearby subtree; global state coordinates distant consumers. Keep state local by default.

sections.title: UI versus remote state
sections.body: UI state describes current interaction. Remote state is server-owned and adds asynchronous freshness, caching, and synchronization concerns.

sections.title: Choosing state placement
sections.body: Choose by ownership, consumers, update frequency, persistence, and source of truth. Context, Redux, and server-state libraries solve different problems.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: Context transports a shared value to a distant consumer.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: Context API and Advanced State Management connects Providing and consuming context, Local versus global state, UI versus remote state.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: A learner must use Context API and Advanced State Management in a new situation. Which approach best demonstrates transferable understanding?

choices.label: Apply Providing and consuming context deliberately, make the relevant inputs and assumptions explicit, and verify the observable result before adding more complexity.
choices.feedback: Transfer comes from using the concept with explicit assumptions and checking the resulting behavior, not from reproducing a memorized example.

choices.label: Skip the underlying model and rely on memorized syntax, even when the result is unclear.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

choices.label: Add more state and abstraction immediately, before identifying the smallest requirement.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

choices.label: Treat the concept as isolated trivia and avoid connecting it to earlier prerequisites.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

questions.explanation: Transfer comes from using the concept with explicit assumptions and checking the resulting behavior, not from reproducing a memorized example.
questions.hint: Choose the option that would still work when the surrounding example changes.

questions.prompt: Which sequence is most reliable when solving a problem involving Context API and Advanced State Management?

choices.label: Start with a framework abstraction and avoid checking the underlying language behavior.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

choices.label: First identify the requirement, then apply the relevant rule from Providing and consuming context, inspect the result, and only then refactor or optimize.
choices.feedback: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.

choices.label: Optimize first, then decide what the code is supposed to do.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

choices.label: Copy an unrelated implementation, hide the assumptions, and test only the happy path.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

questions.explanation: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.
questions.hint: Prefer a sequence that makes failures visible early.

questions.prompt: Which guideline shows the best judgment about when to use Context API and Advanced State Management?

choices.label: Avoid Context API and Advanced State Management completely because all abstractions reduce maintainability.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

choices.label: Use Context API and Advanced State Management when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.
choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.

choices.label: Use Context API and Advanced State Management in every file because more abstraction is always better.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

choices.label: Choose based only on line count, without considering readability, correctness, or future change.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Context API and Advanced State Management.

questions.explanation: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
questions.hint: Choose the option that acknowledges both benefits and trade-offs.
